steps.title: Set the equations equal to form a quadratic
Apply the discriminant condition for no real solutions
steps.content:
For $$Ax^2 + Bx + C = 0$$, no real solutions when discriminant $$B^2 - 4AC < 0$$.
Here $$A=1, B=10, C = a+0.5$$.
So $$10^2 - 4(1)(a+0.5) < 0$$.

steps.title: Solve the inequality for a
steps.content:
$$100 - 4a - 2 < 0$$
$$98 - 4a < 0$$
$$4a > 98$$
$$a > 24.5$$
Since $$a$$ is a positive integer, the least possible value is $$a = 25$$.
The answer is 25.
